user_message: Just got this from Angela Doyle at Contoso Ltd -- I need help figuring out account health. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 14:25 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd account health

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 14:02 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Contoso Ltd

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 16:17 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-K trap (Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-K failure mode (Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-K -- Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
